file.content:
### Discord (`discord/`)

- **Connection**: WebSocket gateway via discord.js Client
- Slash commands must be **deployed** to Discord's API before they appear. Run `pnpm deploy-commands` after any command change. This calls `Routes.applicationCommands(clientId)` globally.
- Interactions have a **3-second deadline**. The adapter auto-defers (`interaction.deferReply()`) on the first `send`/`sendEphemeral`/`sendRich` call to avoid expiry.
- Ephemeral replies defer with `MessageFlags.Ephemeral`. Public replies defer without it. The flag is determined by which method (`send` vs `sendEphemeral`) fires first.
- `sendRich` converts `RichMessage` to a Discord `EmbedBuilder` via `richMessageToEmbed` (exported from `discord/src/adapter.ts`).
- Typing indicator: `sendTyping()` lasts ~10 s; the adapter refreshes it every 8 s.
- Rotating presence statuses cycle every 3 minutes (`ROTATING_STATUSES` array).
- DM welcome embed is sent once per user per process lifetime, gated by the shared `BaseBotAdapter.shouldSendWelcome`.
- Context menu commands ("Summarize with GAIA", "Add as Todo") use `MessageContextMenuCommandInteraction` and always reply ephemeral.
- Required env vars: `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID`

### Slack (`slack/`)

input.old_string:
- Context menu commands ("Summarize with GAIA", "Add as Todo") use `MessageContextMenuCommandInteraction` and always reply ephemeral.
- Required env vars: `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID`
input.new_string:
- Context menu commands ("Summarize with GAIA", "Add as Todo") use `MessageContextMenuCommandInteraction` and always reply ephemeral.
- Media: images, documents, voice notes, and audio attachments are handled via the shared `resolveIncomingMedia` pipeline. `extractDiscordMedia` maps `message.attachments` / `message.stickers` to `IncomingMedia` (kind from MIME via `mediaKindFromMime`, voice notes via the `IsVoiceMessage` flag); bytes download from the public attachment CDN URL (no auth). Works in both DMs and @mentions; video/stickers get a polite reply. Requires the `MessageContent` intent (already set).
- Required env vars: `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, `DISCORD_CLIENT_ID`

content.text:
The Discord client ID is `1508184240297218149`. Implementation is **done, verified, and pushed** (`0ebf1cb5e`).

## What I added
- **`mediaKindFromMime()`** in the shared `media.ts` (image/audio/video/else→document) — reused by Slack later.
- **Discord adapter**: `extractDiscordMedia()` maps `message.attachments` (or stickers) → `IncomingMedia`, downloads from the public CDN URL, and `resolveDiscordMedia()` runs it through the existing shared pipeline. Wired into both `handleDMMessage` and `handleMentionMessage`, with attachments passed via `fileIds`/`fileData`.
- Updated `apps/bots/CLAUDE.md` (Discord is now `image / doc / voice / audio`).
- Verified: type-check ✓, lint ✓, 368 tests ✓, dead-code scan ✓.

No backend changes — it reuses the same `/upload` + `/transcribe` endpoints.

## How to add it to a server and message it

**1. Enable the Message Content intent (one-time, required).** Without this the bot can't read text or attachments.
- [Discord Developer Portal](https://discord.com/developers/applications) → your app → **Bot** → **Privileged Gateway Intents** → toggle **Message Content Intent** ON → Save.

**2. Invite the bot** (this URL uses your client ID from `apps/bots/.env`):
```
https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=1508184240297218149&permissions=117824&scope=bot+applications.commands
```
Open it, pick a server you have **Manage Server** on, authorize. (Permissions = View Channels, Send Messages, Embed Links, Attach Files, Read Message History, Add Reactions.)

**3. Run the bot with commands deployed:**
```bash
pnpm --filter @gaia/bot-discord deploy-commands   # registers /gaia, /auth, etc. (one-time)
nx dev bot-discord                                 # restart so it picks up the new media code
```
It connects automatically using `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` from `.env`.

**4. Link your account (required for media).** DM the bot `/auth`, open the link, connect. Upload/transcribe needs a linked GAIA user — without it you'll get the "link your account" reply.
> Note: your `GAIA_FRONTEND_URL` is `http://localhost:3000`, so the auth link points at localhost — the web app (`nx dev web`) must be running for linking to work.

**5. Test it.** DM the bot, or @mention it in a channel, with:
- an **image** → it describes it
- a **PDF/doc** → it summarizes / answers about it
- a **voice note or audio** → transcribed → answered
- **media + a caption** → caption becomes the prompt
- a **video/sticker** → polite "can't read this yet" reply

Want me to do Slack next (it needs the `files:read` scope + a reinstall, which I'll walk you through), or leave it here?

content.content:
=== bot.py _bot_rate_limit_notice ===
def _bot_rate_limit_notice(chunk: dict) -> str | None:
    """Render a web-only rate-limit card as a plain-text notice for bots.

    Rate limits are streamed as a ``tool_data`` card for the web UI to render.
    Bots drop ``tool_data``, so without this they'd silently swallow the limit.
    Returns the user-facing notice, or ``None`` if ``chunk`` isn't such a card.

    The upgrade link is emitted as CommonMark ``[label](url)``; each bot adapter
    localises it to its platform's link syntax (WhatsApp ``label (url)``, Slack
    ``<url|label>``, Telegram keeps ``[label](url)``).
    """
    tool_data = chunk.get("tool_data")
    if not isinstance(tool_data, dict) or tool_data.get("tool_name") != "rate_limit_data":
        return None

    card = tool_data.get("data") or {}
    feature = str(card.get("feature") or "this feature").replace("_", " ")
    notice = f"⏳ You've reached your {feature} limit. Please try again later."

def _bot_rate_limit_notice(chunk: dict) -> str | None:
    """Render a web-only rate-limit card as a plain-text notice for bots.

    Rate limits are streamed as a ``tool_data`` card for the web UI to render.
    Bots drop ``tool_data``, so without this they'd silently swallow the limit.
    Returns the user-facing notice, or ``None`` if ``chunk`` isn't such a card.

    The upgrade link is emitted as CommonMark ``[label](url)``; each bot adapter
    localises it to its platform's link syntax (WhatsApp ``label (url)``, Slack
    ``<url|label>``, Telegram keeps ``[label](url)``).
    """
    tool_data = chunk.get("tool_data")
    if not isinstance(tool_data, dict) or tool_data.get("tool_name") != "rate_limit_data":
        return None

    card = tool_data.get("data") or {}
    feature = str(card.get("feature") or "this feature").replace("_", " ")
    notice = f"⏳ You've reached your {feature} limit. Please try again later."

    # Nudge an upgrade only for non-Pro users (Pro is the top tier).
    if card.get("current_plan") != "pro":
        pricing_url = f"{settings.FRONTEND_URL}/pricing"
        notice += f" [Upgrade to Pro]({pricing_url}) for higher limits."
    return notice

input.description: Type-check and lint the three media adapters

attachment.prompt: i feel like this is a side effect of just a bad pattern

content.text:
You're right, and it's worth naming precisely. What I just did is a band-aid on a divergence, not a fix for the divergence itself.

## The bad pattern

"User hit a rate limit" gets turned into a user-facing message in **two completely separate places, in two languages**:

| | Chat / agent tools | Media upload + transcribe |
|---|---|---|
| Enforced by | `@with_rate_limiting` (tool decorator) | `@tiered_rate_limit` / inline `check_and_increment` (HTTP endpoint) |
| Surfaced as | a `rate_limit_data` **stream card** | a plain **HTTP 429** |
| Rendered to text by | `_bot_rate_limit_notice` — **Python**, `bot.py` | `friendlyMediaError` — **TypeScript**, `media.ts` |
| Result | rich: feature name, plan-gated, `[Upgrade to Pro](…/pricing)` | terse: hardcoded string, no URL, no plan awareness |

Both ultimately come from the *same* `RateLimitExceededException`. But because tools stream a card and REST endpoints throw 429, the message gets re-implemented on each side and they drift. The missing URL is just the most visible drift — the wording, the plan-gating, and the `/pricing` path are all duplicated too. My patch (appending the URL in `friendlyMediaError`) makes the two *look* closer but there are still two renderers that will drift again.

## The actual fix (single source of truth)

The notice should be authored **once, on the backend**, and both surfaces should just carry/display it:

1. **One backend renderer** — extract a `build_rate_limit_notice(feature, current_plan, frontend_url)` helper. `_bot_rate_limit_notice` calls it; `RateLimitExceededException` puts its output (plus `current_plan`) into the 429 `detail`.
2. **Bot just displays** — `friendlyMediaError`'s 429 branch surfaces `err.response.detail.notice`, generic string only as a last-resort fallback. No wording/URL/plan logic in TS at all.
3. **One render path** — route media `reply` outcomes through `renderForPlatform` (like streamed chat already is), so the upgrade link is localized identically everywhere instead of the media path sending raw text.

After that, wording + URL + plan-gating live in exactly one function, and chat vs media are guaranteed identical.

## What's committed where

My bot-side patch is **made but not committed** (type-check + lint pass). It's a strict improvement — the URL shows today — but it keeps the two-renderer smell you're pointing at.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## GAIA Bot Error-to-Message Inventory

I've completed a comprehensive read-only mapping of **every function that converts errors/limits into user-facing bot messages**. Here's the complete inventory:

---

### **1. CORE ERROR FORMATTING FUNCTION**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/formatters.ts:429-485`

**Function:** `formatBotError(error: unknown): string`
- **Purpose:** Single canonical error formatter for ALL bot platforms
- **Logic flow:**
  1. Extracts HTTP status code from `GaiaApiError.status` or `error.response?.status`
  2. Maps status codes to user messages
  3. Falls back to error message pattern matching
  4. Logs unhandled errors to console
  5. Returns generic fallback message

- **User-facing strings produced:**
  - Status 401: `"❌ Authentication required. Use \`/auth\` to link your account."`
  - Status 404: `"❌ Not found. Please check the ID and try again."`
  - Status 429: `"⏳ You're sending messages too fast. Please wait a moment and try again."`
  - Timeout patterns: `"⏳ The request timed out. The server may be busy — please try again in a moment."`
  - Connection lost: `"❌ Connection lost before receiving a response. Please try again."`
  - AI taking too long: `"⏳ Your request is taking longer than usual. Try a simpler question or wait a moment and try again."`
  - Backend unavailable (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT): `"🔌 The GAIA backend is unavailable. Please try again in a moment."`
  - Connection interrupted (ECONNRESET, socket hang up): `"🔌 Connection interrupted. Please try again."`
  - Incomplete stream: `"⚠️ Response was incomplete. Please try again."`
  - Fallback: `"❌ Something went wrong. Please try again later."`

- **Called from:**
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:284` (inside error handler)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:289` (in outer try-catch)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/commands.ts:37,54,67,88,101,117,130,147,160,200` (all 10 command handlers wrap in formatBotError)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/adapter/base.ts:46` (imported)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/adapter/base.ts:349` (in dispatchCommand error handler)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/settings.ts:114` (settings command catch block)

- **Rendering:** Goes through `renderForPlatform()` per platform when called from adapters

---

### **2. AUTHENTICATION LINK MESSAGE FUNCTION**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/formatters.ts:374-381`

**Function:** `buildAuthLinkMessage(authUrl: string): string`
- **Purpose:** Single canonical "link your account" prompt used everywhere (`/auth` command + all auth errors)
- **User-facing string:**
  ```
  🔗 **Link your account to GAIA**

  Tap the link below to sign in and link your account:
  {authUrl}

  After linking, you'll be able to use all GAIA commands!
  ```

- **Called from:**
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/auth.ts:35` (line 35 in auth command execute)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/status.ts:34` (status command, if not linked but token generation succeeds)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/settings.ts:45` (settings command fallback)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:273` (in _handleStream auth error recovery)

- **Rendering:** Always explicitly sent through `renderForPlatform()` and `parse_mode: HTML` on Telegram

---

### **3. STREAMING CHAT ERROR HANDLER**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:70-291`

**Functions:** 
- `_handleStream()` (internal, lines 70-291)
- `handleStreamingChat()` (public entry point, lines 296-377)

**Error routing logic (lines 265-286):**
1. Checks if error message is `"not_authenticated"` specifically
2. If auth error AND `onAuthError` callback exists:
   - Calls `gaia.createLinkToken()`
   - Passes auth URL to `onAuthError()` callback
   - On token creation failure: calls `onGenericError("Failed to generate auth link. Please try /auth again.")`
3. Otherwise: calls `onGenericError(formatBotError(error))`

**Special timeout/keepalive messages (lines 287-399):**
- Inactivity timeout (5 min, no data): `"The AI is taking longer than expected. Please try a simpler request or try again later."` or `"Connection timeout - no response from server. Please try again."`
- Received keepalive but stream ended: `"The AI is processing your request but hasn't responded yet. Please try again."`
- No keepalive, stream ended: `"Connection lost before receiving a response. Please try again."`
- Stream error (ECONNRESET/socket hang up/timeout): Custom messages per error type

- **Called from:**
  - All platform adapters: Discord, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp `/gaia` handlers
  - Takes `onAuthError` and `onGenericError` callbacks from each adapter

- **Rendering:** All text flows through `PLATFORM_MARKDOWN[platform]()` at line 89-96 (centralized markdown conversion chokepoint)

---

### **4. MEDIA ERROR HANDLER**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:74-99`

**Function:** `friendlyMediaError(kind: MediaKind, err: unknown, pricingUrl?: string): string`
- **Purpose:** Maps upload/transcribe failures to user-facing replies
- **Inputs:** media kind (image/audio/video/document/sticker), error object, optional pricing URL
- **User-facing strings:**
  - Status 401/403: `"I need you to link your GAIA account first before I can read attachments. Send /auth to get started."`
  - Status 413 (too large): `"That {kind} is too large for me to process. Please share a smaller file."`
  - Status 415 (unsupported format): `"I can't read this kind of {kind} yet. Try a common format like JPG, PNG, PDF, or an OGG voice note."`
  - Status 429 (rate limit): `"You've reached your {voice transcription OR file upload} limit for now. Please try again later, or upgrade your plan for higher limits."` (+ pricing URL if provided)
  - Default: `"Something went wrong while processing your attachment. Please try again in a moment."`

- **Called from:**
  - `/apps/bots/discord/src/adapter.ts` (in media download error catch block)

- **Rendering:** Sent raw as `action: "reply"` text, adapters send through platform API

---

### **5. UNSUPPORTED MEDIA MESSAGE**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:59-67`

**Function:** `unsupportedMediaMessage(kind: string): string`
- **Purpose:** Reject video/sticker uploads politely without downloading
- **User-facing string:**
  ```
  I can't process {videos/stickers/other messages} yet — please send your message as text, an image, a document, or a voice note. Type /help for available commands.
  ```

- **Called from:**
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:166` (inside `processBotMedia`, line 166 for video/sticker detection)

- **Rendering:** Sent as `action: "reply"` from `processBotMedia()`

---

### **6. MEDIA SIZE LIMIT MESSAGES** (inline in `processBotMedia`)

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:159-234`

**Location:** Inside `processBotMedia()` function

**Size limit error strings:**
- Audio over 25 MB (line 172-178):
  ```
  That voice note is too large to transcribe (limit: {limit} MB). Please send a shorter message.
  ```

- File over 10 MB (line 206-212):
  ```
  That file is too large to process (limit: {limit} MB). Please share a smaller file.
  ```

- Audio transcription failed/empty (line 191-195):
  ```
  I couldn't understand that audio. Could you try recording again or sending a text message?
  ```

- **Rendering:** All returned as `action: "reply"` from `processBotMedia()`

---

### **7. COMMAND-SPECIFIC ERROR HANDLERS**

#### **A. Auth Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/auth.ts:29-41`
- On token creation failure: `"❌ Failed to generate auth link. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **B. Status Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/status.ts:42-44`
- On any error: `"Error checking status. Please try again."`
- On auth check success but not authenticated fallback: `"❌ Not linked yet. Use /auth to link your account."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **C. Settings Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/settings.ts:35-115`
- On auth token generation failure: `"❌ Not linked yet. Use /auth to link your account."`
- On any other error (line 114): uses `formatBotError(error)`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()` → `renderForPlatform()`

#### **D. Unlink Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/unlink.ts:34-37`
- On unlink failure: `"❌ Failed to unlink your account. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **E. Stop Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/stop.ts:20-21`
- On reset failure: `"❌ Failed to stop. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **F. New Conversation Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/commands.ts:243-253`
- On reset failure: `"Failed to start a new conversation. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** Returned as string (caller decides how to send)

#### **G. Todo/Workflow Commands**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/commands.ts:29-228`
- All 10 command handlers (`handleTodoList`, `handleTodoCreate`, `handleTodoComplete`, `handleTodoDelete`, `handleWorkflowList`, `handleWorkflowGet`, `handleWorkflowCreate`, `handleWorkflowExecute`, `handleWorkflowDelete`, `handleConversationList`) wrap errors with `formatBotError(error)`
- **Rendering:** Returned as string (caller decides how to send)

---

### **8. STREAMING CHAT SPECIFIC MESSAGES**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/api/index.ts:223-471` (in `_chatStreamInternal`)

These are embedded directly in stream error handlers:

- Line 289: No keepalive + timeout → `"Connection timeout - no response from server. Please try again."`
- Line 290: Keepalive received but no content → `"The AI is taking longer than expected. Please try a simpler request or try again later."`
- Line 393-395: Stream ends but keepalive received → `"The AI is processing your request but hasn't responded yet. Please try again."`
- Line 397-401: Stream ends with no keepalive or content → `"Connection lost before receiving a response. Please try again."`
- Line 431: Socket timeout → `"Request timed out. The server may be busy - please try again."`
- Line 429: ECONNRESET/socket hang up → `"Connection interrupted. Please try again."`

---

### **COMPLETE CALL CHAIN SUMMARY**

```
User sends message
  ↓
Platform adapter (Discord/Slack/Telegram/WhatsApp)
  ├─ If slash command → dispatchCommand() 
  │   ├─ Calls unified BotCommand.execute()
  │   ├─ Command handler wraps API calls with try/catch
  │   ├─ On error → formatBotError() → target.sendEphemeral()
  │   └─ Rendered via renderForPlatform()
  │
  ├─ If chat/mention → handleStreamingChat()
  │   ├─ Calls gaia.chatStream()
  │   ├─ On "not_authenticated" → buildAuthLinkMessage() → onAuthError()
  │   ├─ On other error → formatBotError() → onGenericError()
  │   └─ All text rendered via PLATFORM_MARKDOWN[platform]()
  │
  └─ If media message → resolveIncomingMedia()
      ├─ Calls processBotMedia()
      ├─ On unsupported kind → unsupportedMediaMessage()
      ├─ On size limit → inline size error messages
      ├─ On transcribe failure → "I couldn't understand that audio..."
      ├─ On upload/transcribe API error → friendlyMediaError()
      └─ Returned as MediaOutcome → adapter sends via platform API
```

---

### **DUPLICATION & DIVERGENCE ANALYSIS**

**Duplication Identified:**

1. **Auth link generation failures** (TWO paths):
   - `/auth` command (line 38): `"❌ Failed to generate auth link. Please try again."`
   - Streaming (line 280): `"Failed to generate auth link. Please try /auth again."`
   - **Difference:** Streaming version suggests `/auth` command explicitly
   - **Why:** Streaming context doesn't show command syntax

2. **Not linked status check failures** (THREE paths):
   - `/status` command (line 38): `"❌ Not linked yet. Use /auth to link your account."`
   - `/settings` command (line 53): `"❌ Not linked yet. Use /auth to link your account."` (exact same)
   - Fallback in `/settings` (line 45): Different message with auth URL attempt
   - **Consolidation opportunity:** These two are identical and could be centralized

3. **Generic "try again" messages**:
   - Status check (line 43): `"Error checking status. Please try again."`
   - Unlink (line 36): `"❌ Failed to unlink your account. Please try again."`
   - Stop (line 21): `"❌ Failed to stop. Please try again."`
   - New conversation (line 251): `"Failed to start a new conversation. Please try again."`
   - **Pattern:** Inconsistent emoji prefix, slight wording variations

**Divergence Identified:**

1. **Rendering paths:** 
   - `formatBotError()` goes through `renderForPlatform()` (markdown conversion)
   - `friendlyMediaError()` sent raw by adapters (no centralized rendering)
   - **Impact:** Media errors might not render correctly on Telegram/Slack if they contain markdown

2. **Error logging:**
   - `formatBotError()` logs unhandled errors to console (line 483)
   - Streaming errors NOT logged by handler (logging deferred to adapter)
   - **Impact:** Some errors may not be visible in logs

3. **Platform-specific auth messages:**
   - `/status` and `/settings` include bare auth URLs (no markdown link)
   - `buildAuthLinkMessage()` also uses bare URLs intentionally
   - **Why:** URLs stay visible & copy-pasteable; markdown links hidden on some platforms

---

### **KEY FILES & LINE REFERENCES**

| File | Function | Lines | Purpose |
|------|----------|-------|---------|
| `formatters.ts` | `formatBotError` | 429-485 | Central error → message mapper |
| `formatters.ts` | `buildAuthLinkMessage` | 374-381 | Auth link prompt generator |
| `streaming.ts` | `_handleStream` | 265-286 | Streaming error router (auth vs generic) |
| `streaming.ts` | `_chatStreamInternal` | 287-401 | Timeout & keepalive messages |
| `media.ts` | `processBotMedia` | 159-234 | Media validation & error messages |
| `media.ts` | `friendlyMediaError` | 74-99 | Upload/transcribe failure formatter |
| `media.ts` | `unsupportedMediaMessage` | 59-67 | Unsupported media kind message |
| `api/index.ts` | `_chatStreamInternal` | 223-471 | Chat stream error handling |
| `commands.ts` | All handlers | 29-253 | Command error wrapping (10 functions) |
| `adapter/base.ts` | `dispatchCommand` | 266-356 | Command dispatch error handler |
| `adapter/base.ts` | `resolveIncomingMedia` | 428-440 | Media message handler |
| `auth.ts` | `authCommand.execute` | 29-41 | /auth error handling |
| `status.ts` | `statusCommand.execute` | 42-44 | /status error handling |
| `settings.ts` | `settingsCommand.execute` | 114 | /settings error handling |
| `unlink.ts` | `unlinkCommand.execute` | 34-37 | /unlink error handling |
| `stop.ts` | `stopCommand.execute` | 20-21 | /stop error handling |

---

### **RENDERING CHAINING**

```
formatBotError() + buildAuthLinkMessage()
  ↓ (when called from commands/streaming)
renderForPlatform(text, platform)
  ↓ (applies PLATFORM_MARKDOWN[platform])
PLATFORM_MARKDOWN map:
  - discord → convertToDiscordMarkdown()
  - slack → convertToSlackMrkdwn()
  - telegram → convertToTelegramHtml()
  - whatsapp → convertToWhatsAppMarkdown()
  ↓
Platform adapter sendEphemeral() / send() / reply()
```

Media errors **bypass** this chain (sent raw by adapters), creating a divergence.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the complete picture. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## GAIA Bot Error-to-Message Inventory

I've completed a comprehensive read-only mapping of **every function that converts errors/limits into user-facing bot messages**. Here's the complete inventory:

---

### **1. CORE ERROR FORMATTING FUNCTION**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/formatters.ts:429-485`

**Function:** `formatBotError(error: unknown): string`
- **Purpose:** Single canonical error formatter for ALL bot platforms
- **Logic flow:**
  1. Extracts HTTP status code from `GaiaApiError.status` or `error.response?.status`
  2. Maps status codes to user messages
  3. Falls back to error message pattern matching
  4. Logs unhandled errors to console
  5. Returns generic fallback message

- **User-facing strings produced:**
  - Status 401: `"❌ Authentication required. Use \`/auth\` to link your account."`
  - Status 404: `"❌ Not found. Please check the ID and try again."`
  - Status 429: `"⏳ You're sending messages too fast. Please wait a moment and try again."`
  - Timeout patterns: `"⏳ The request timed out. The server may be busy — please try again in a moment."`
  - Connection lost: `"❌ Connection lost before receiving a response. Please try again."`
  - AI taking too long: `"⏳ Your request is taking longer than usual. Try a simpler question or wait a moment and try again."`
  - Backend unavailable (ECONNREFUSED, ETIMEDOUT): `"🔌 The GAIA backend is unavailable. Please try again in a moment."`
  - Connection interrupted (ECONNRESET, socket hang up): `"🔌 Connection interrupted. Please try again."`
  - Incomplete stream: `"⚠️ Response was incomplete. Please try again."`
  - Fallback: `"❌ Something went wrong. Please try again later."`

- **Called from:**
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:284` (inside error handler)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:289` (in outer try-catch)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/commands.ts:37,54,67,88,101,117,130,147,160,200` (all 10 command handlers wrap in formatBotError)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/adapter/base.ts:46` (imported)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/adapter/base.ts:349` (in dispatchCommand error handler)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/settings.ts:114` (settings command catch block)

- **Rendering:** Goes through `renderForPlatform()` per platform when called from adapters

---

### **2. AUTHENTICATION LINK MESSAGE FUNCTION**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/formatters.ts:374-381`

**Function:** `buildAuthLinkMessage(authUrl: string): string`
- **Purpose:** Single canonical "link your account" prompt used everywhere (`/auth` command + all auth errors)
- **User-facing string:**
  ```
  🔗 **Link your account to GAIA**

  Tap the link below to sign in and link your account:
  {authUrl}

  After linking, you'll be able to use all GAIA commands!
  ```

- **Called from:**
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/auth.ts:35` (line 35 in auth command execute)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/status.ts:34` (status command, if not linked but token generation succeeds)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/settings.ts:45` (settings command fallback)
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:273` (in _handleStream auth error recovery)

- **Rendering:** Always explicitly sent through `renderForPlatform()` and `parse_mode: HTML` on Telegram

---

### **3. STREAMING CHAT ERROR HANDLER**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/streaming.ts:70-291`

**Functions:** 
- `_handleStream()` (internal, lines 70-291)
- `handleStreamingChat()` (public entry point, lines 296-377)

**Error routing logic (lines 265-286):**
1. Checks if error message is `"not_authenticated"` specifically
2. If auth error AND `onAuthError` callback exists:
   - Calls `gaia.createLinkToken()`
   - Passes auth URL to `onAuthError()` callback
   - On token creation failure: calls `onGenericError("Failed to generate auth link. Please try /auth again.")`
3. Otherwise: calls `onGenericError(formatBotError(error))`

**Special timeout/keepalive messages (lines 287-399):**
- Inactivity timeout (5 min, no data): `"The AI is taking longer than expected. Please try a simpler request or try again later."` or `"Connection timeout - no response from server. Please try again."`
- Received keepalive but stream ended: `"The AI is processing your request but hasn't responded yet. Please try again."`
- No keepalive, stream ended: `"Connection lost before receiving a response. Please try again."`
- Stream error (ECONNRESET/socket hang up/timeout): Custom messages per error type

- **Called from:**
  - All platform adapters: Discord, Slack, Telegram, WhatsApp `/gaia` handlers
  - Takes `onAuthError` and `onGenericError` callbacks from each adapter

- **Rendering:** All text flows through `PLATFORM_MARKDOWN[platform]()` at line 89-96 (centralized markdown conversion chokepoint)

---

### **4. MEDIA ERROR HANDLER**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:74-99`

**Function:** `friendlyMediaError(kind: MediaKind, err: unknown, pricingUrl?: string): string`
- **Purpose:** Maps upload/transcribe failures to user-facing replies
- **Inputs:** media kind (image/audio/video/document/sticker), error object, optional pricing URL
- **User-facing strings:**
  - Status 401/403: `"I need you to link your GAIA account first before I can read attachments. Send /auth to get started."`
  - Status 413 (too large): `"That {kind} is too large for me to process. Please share a smaller file."`
  - Status 415 (unsupported format): `"I can't read this kind of {kind} yet. Try a common format like JPG, PNG, PDF, or an OGG voice note."`
  - Status 429 (rate limit): `"You've reached your {voice transcription OR file upload} limit for now. Please try again later, or upgrade your plan for higher limits."` (+ pricing URL if provided)
  - Default: `"Something went wrong while processing your attachment. Please try again in a moment."`

- **Called from:**
  - `/apps/bots/discord/src/adapter.ts` (in media download error catch block)

- **Rendering:** Sent raw as `action: "reply"` text, adapters send through platform API

---

### **5. UNSUPPORTED MEDIA MESSAGE**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:59-67`

**Function:** `unsupportedMediaMessage(kind: string): string`
- **Purpose:** Reject video/sticker uploads politely without downloading
- **User-facing string:**
  ```
  I can't process {videos/stickers/other messages} yet — please send your message as text, an image, a document, or a voice note. Type /help for available commands.
  ```

- **Called from:**
  - `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:166` (inside `processBotMedia`, line 166 for video/sticker detection)

- **Rendering:** Sent as `action: "reply"` from `processBotMedia()`

---

### **6. MEDIA SIZE LIMIT MESSAGES** (inline in `processBotMedia`)

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/media.ts:159-234`

**Location:** Inside `processBotMedia()` function

**Size limit error strings:**
- Audio over 25 MB (line 172-178):
  ```

  ```

- File over 10 MB (line 206-212):
  ```
  That file is too large to process (limit: {limit} MB). Please share a smaller file.
  ```

- Audio transcription failed/empty (line 191-195):
  ```
  I couldn't understand that audio. Could you try recording again or sending a text message?
  ```

- **Rendering:** All returned as `action: "reply"` from `processBotMedia()`

---

### **7. COMMAND-SPECIFIC ERROR HANDLERS**

#### **A. Auth Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/auth.ts:29-41`
- On token creation failure: `"❌ Failed to generate auth link. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **B. Status Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/status.ts:42-44`
- On any error: `"Error checking status. Please try again."`
- On auth check success but not authenticated fallback: `"❌ Not linked yet. Use /auth to link your account."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **C. Settings Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/settings.ts:35-115`
- On auth token generation failure: `"❌ Not linked yet. Use /auth to link your account."`
- On any other error (line 114): uses `formatBotError(error)`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()` → `renderForPlatform()`

#### **D. Unlink Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/unlink.ts:34-37`
- On unlink failure: `"❌ Failed to unlink your account. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **E. Stop Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/commands/stop.ts:20-21`
- On reset failure: `"❌ Failed to stop. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** via `target.sendEphemeral()`

#### **F. New Conversation Command**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/commands.ts:243-253`
- On reset failure: `"Failed to start a new conversation. Please try again."`
- **Rendering:** Returned as string (caller decides how to send)

#### **G. Todo/Workflow Commands**
**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/utils/commands.ts:29-228`
- All 10 command handlers (`handleTodoList`, `handleTodoCreate`, `handleTodoComplete`, `handleTodoDelete`, `handleWorkflowList`, `handleWorkflowGet`, `handleWorkflowCreate`, `handleWorkflowExecute`, `handleWorkflowDelete`, `handleConversationList`) wrap errors with `formatBotError(error)`
- **Rendering:** Returned as string (caller decides how to send)

---

### **8. STREAMING CHAT SPECIFIC MESSAGES**

**File:** `/libs/shared/ts/src/bots/api/index.ts:223-471` (in `_chatStreamInternal`)

These are embedded directly in stream error handlers:

- Line 289: No keepalive + timeout → `"Connection timeout - no response from server. Please try again."`
- Line 290: Keepalive received but no content → `"The AI is taking longer than expected. Please try a simpler request or try again later."`
- Line 393-395: Stream ends but keepalive received → `"The AI is processing your request but hasn't responded yet. Please try again."`
- Line 397-401: Stream ends with no keepalive or content → `"Connection lost before receiving a response. Please try again."`
- Line 431: Socket timeout → `"Request timed out. The server may be busy - please try again."`
- Line 429: ECONNRESET/socket hang up → `"Connection interrupted. Please try again."`

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information needed to create a comprehensive mapping. Let me compile my findings:

---

## Complete Error and Rate-Limit Surface Mapping for GAIA

### Backend: Exception & Rate-Limit Flow

#### 1. **RateLimitExceededException Definition & HTTP 429 Response**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/api/v1/middleware/tiered_rate_limiter.py:44-64`

- **Exception Class:** `RateLimitExceededException(HTTPException)` 
- **HTTP Status:** 429
- **Detail Payload Structure:**
  ```python
  detail = {
      "error": "rate_limit_exceeded",
      "feature": feature,
      "message": f"Rate limit exceeded for {feature}",
      "plan_required": plan_required,      # Optional, only if upgrade needed
      "reset_time": reset_time.isoformat() # Optional, ISO format datetime
  }
  ```
- **Where Raised:**
  - `/tiered_rate_limiter.py:107` — in `check_and_increment()` before incrementing
  - `/tiered_rate_limiter.py:139-141` — in `check_and_increment()` during concurrent check (WATCH/retry loop)
  - Both paths set `plan_required` to `"pro"` only if FREE user hits a plan-gated feature
  - `reset_time` always set to the appropriate window's reset time

---

#### 2. **Rate Limit Middleware Exception Handler**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/core/middleware.py:26-44`

- **Handler Function:** `rate_limit_handler(request, exc)` 
- **Triggered By:** `slowapi.errors.RateLimitExceeded` (global IP-based rate limiter)
- **Response Payload:**
  ```json
  {
      "error": "rate_limit_exceeded",
      "detail": "<str(exc)>",
      "retry_after": <int or None>
  }
  ```
- **Note:** This is **distinct from** tiered_limiter exceptions — it's for the global IP-level limiter (SlowAPI)

---

#### 3. **AppError Exception Handler**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/core/app_factory.py:72-79`

- **Handler:** `app_error_handler(request, exc)` 
- **Response Payload:** Calls `exc.to_dict()` which returns:
  ```python
  {
      "message": self.message,
      "why": self.why,          # if present
      "fix": self.fix,          # if present
      ...meta fields...
  }
  ```
- **Status Code:** Taken from `exc.status_code`
- **Location of Definition:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/utils/errors.py:25-43`

---

#### 4. **Two Decorator Paths for Rate Limiting**

##### **Path 1: Endpoint Decorator (HTTP Handlers)**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/decorators/rate_limiting.py:215-275`

- **Decorator:** `@tiered_rate_limit(feature_key: str)`
- **Used On:** HTTP endpoints (e.g., `/bot/transcribe` at line 489 in bot.py)
- **Behavior:**
  - Extracts user from function args/kwargs
  - Calls `tiered_limiter.check_and_increment()` before executing endpoint
  - Raises `RateLimitExceededException` (which FastAPI converts to HTTP 429)
  - No tool_data card emission (HTTP-only)

##### **Path 2: LangGraph Tool Decorator**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/decorators/rate_limiting.py:34-212`

- **Decorator:** `@with_rate_limiting(feature_key, count_tokens, bypass_for_system)`
- **Used On:** Tools inside LangGraph agent functions
- **Behavior:**
  - Catches `RateLimitExceededException` and converts to `LangChainRateLimitException`
  - **Emits tool_data card via LangGraph stream writer** (lines 137-156):
    ```python
    writer({
        "tool_data": {
            "tool_name": "rate_limit_data",
            "tool_category": "system",
            "data": {
                "feature": actual_feature_key,
                "plan_required": detail_dict.get("plan_required"),
                "reset_time": reset_time,
                "current_plan": user_plan.value
            },
            "timestamp": datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
        }
    })
    ```
  - Then raises `LangChainRateLimitException` to agent (non-fatal for SSE)

---

#### 5. **Chat Stream Error Emission**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/services/chat_service.py:469-476`

- **Catch Block:** In `_run_chat_stream()` at lines 469-476
- **Error Event Shape:**
  ```python
  {"error": str(e)}  # Plain string serialization
  ```
- **SSE Format:** 
  ```
  data: {"error": "..."}\n\n
  ```
- **Published Before:** `stream_manager.set_error()` (critical ordering comment at line 472-474)

---

#### 6. **Tool Rate-Limit Text Builder for Bots**
**File:** `/Users/aryan/Projects/GAIA/gaia-feat-whatsapp-media-support/apps/api/app/api/v1/endpoints/bot.py:53-76`

- **Function:** `_bot_rate_limit_notice(chunk: dict) -> str | None`
- **Input:** SSE chunk containing `tool_data`

      "error": "rate_limit_exceeded",

      "reset_time": reset_time.isoformat() # Optional, ISO format datetime
  }

  - `/tiered_rate_limiter.py:139-141` — in `check_and_increment()` during concurrent check (WATCH/retry loop)

  - `reset_time` always set to the appropriate window's reset time

Deep exploration done. Here's the complete picture and the cleanest architecture.

## What's actually there today

A bot user can see an error/limit through **five independent mechanisms**, with presentation split across two languages and two layers:

| Surface | Where the text is built | Goes through `renderForPlatform`? | Has pricing link? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Chat/command errors | `formatBotError()` (TS) | ✅ yes | n/a |
| Auth prompt | `buildAuthLinkMessage()` (TS) | ✅ yes | n/a |
| **Chat/tool rate limit** | `_bot_rate_limit_notice()` (**Python**, `bot.py`) | ✅ (streamed text) | ✅ yes |
| **Media rate limit** | `friendlyMediaError()` (TS, `media.ts`) | ❌ **raw** | ❌ no |
| Media errors (413/415/etc.) | `friendlyMediaError()` (TS) | ❌ **raw** | n/a |

## The root smell

**Presentation logic is duplicated and mislocated.** Specifically:

1. **Two error→text mappers on the bot side** (`formatBotError` and `friendlyMediaError`) that overlap on 401/429/generic but diverge in wording *and* rendering.
2. **Rate-limit text is built twice** — once on the **backend** (`_bot_rate_limit_notice`, rich + link + plan-aware) and once in the **bot** (`friendlyMediaError`, terse + no link). Same event, two renderers → drift. The missing URL is just the visible symptom.
3. **The two backend payloads don't match**: the stream `rate_limit_data` card carries `current_plan`, but the HTTP **429 detail does not** — so a bot receiving a 429 *can't* reproduce the card's plan-gated logic even if it wanted to.
4. **Inconsistent render path**: everything except media replies flows through `renderForPlatform`; media replies send raw.
5. **The backend renders bot-specific text** (`_bot_rate_limit_notice` lives in the bot endpoint) — but the **web already does it the right way**: the backend hands web structured data (`{feature, plan_required, reset_time}`) and the web renders its own `RateLimitCard`/toast. Bots are the odd one out.

## The cleanest architecture

The principle the web already follows: **backend owns DATA, each client owns PRESENTATION — one error mapper and one rate-limit renderer per client, one render path.**

```
                 ┌─────────────── BACKEND = structured data only ───────────────┐
   rate limit →  │  ONE shape on BOTH paths:                                     │
   (tool OR      │  { error:"rate_limit_exceeded", feature, current_plan,        │
    endpoint)    │    plan_required?, reset_time? }                              │
                 │   • stream: rate_limit_data card (already structured)         │
                 │   • HTTP:   429 detail  ← ADD current_plan to match           │
                 │   • DELETE _bot_rate_limit_notice (presentation leaves backend)│
                 └───────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────┘
                          ┌──────────────────┴───────────────────┐
                          ▼                                       ▼
                ┌──── WEB (unchanged) ────┐         ┌──────── BOTS (TS) ────────┐
                │ RateLimitCard / toast    │         │ ONE presentation layer:    │
                │ reads {feature,plan,...}  │        │ • renderRateLimitNotice(    │
                └──────────────────────────┘        │     data, frontendUrl)      │
                                                     │ • formatBotError(err,plat)  │
                                                     │   ↳ detects rate-limit 429  │
                                                     │     body → delegates to     │
                                                     │     renderRateLimitNotice   │
                                                     │ • friendlyMediaError = thin │
                                                     │   wrapper: 413/415 media     │
                                                     │   copy, else → formatBotError│
                                                     │ • EVERYTHING → renderForPlatform│
                                                     └─────────────────────────────┘
```

**Concrete components after the refactor:**

- **`renderRateLimitNotice(data, frontendUrl)`** — the single bot rate-limit text builder. CommonMark `[Upgrade to Pro](frontendUrl/pricing)` gated on `current_plan !== "pro"`, plus feature name + optional reset time. The pricing URL comes from the **bot's own** `GaiaClient.frontendUrl` (today the backend uses `settings.FRONTEND_URL` — a separate source that can drift from the bot's `GAIA_FRONTEND_URL`).
- **`formatBotError(err, platform, opts?)`** stays *the* error mapper, and becomes the dispatcher: a 429 whose body is `rate_limit_exceeded` → `renderRateLimitNotice`; a slowapi/global 429 (`retry_after`) → "slow down"; everything else as today.
- **`friendlyMediaError` shrinks to a thin wrapper** — keeps only media-specific copy (413 "too large", 415 "unsupported format"), delegates 401/429/generic to the shared functions. No more duplicated 429/auth logic.
- **`GaiaApiError` gains `.detail`** (the parsed response body) so the bot can read `{feature, current_plan}` off a 429.
- **Media replies routed through `renderForPlatform`** — kills the last raw-text path.
- **Bot stream forwarder** forwards the `rate_limit_data` card to bots as structured data instead of pre-rendering text; `handleStreamingChat` renders it via `renderRateLimitNotice`.

**Net deletions/merges:** `_bot_rate_limit_notice` (backend) deleted; two error mappers → one; two rate-limit renderers → one; one render path.

## Honest scoping

The **fully clean version** (forwarding structured cards to bots + deleting backend presentation) touches the SSE bot protocol + `handleStreamingChat` + `GaiaApiError` + the 429 detail + tests. It's the right end state but it's a real refactor.

There's a **smaller 80%-version** that fixes the user-visible bug and removes the *worst* duplication without touching the stream protocol: add `current_plan` to the 429 detail + carry it on `GaiaApiError.detail` + have `friendlyMediaError`/`formatBotError` call **one shared `renderRateLimitNotice`** for 429s (so the media path gets the exact same notice + link as chat). Leaves `_bot_rate_limit_notice` on the backend for the stream path (one remaining renderer, but it'd produce identical text via the shared builder if I mirror it — or accept it as the one allowed exception).

Note: my earlier band-aid (the `pricingUrl` param + `getPricingUrl()` + 3 call-site edits) is still **uncommitted** in the working tree — it'll be folded into / replaced by whichever option you pick.

message.content: alos how will this fix the actual errors like apart from 429 rate limited shit?

content.text:
Sharp question — and I should be honest: the rate-limit work, *as I scoped it*, makes general errors more **consistent**, but it does **not** by itself make them more **accurate**. Here's the distinction.

## What "full clean" already does for non-429 errors

Right now there are **two error mappers that disagree**:

| Status | `formatBotError` (chat/commands) | `friendlyMediaError` (media) |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | "Authentication required. Use `/auth`" | "link your GAIA account first… Send /auth" |
| timeout/network | rich, specific strings | falls to generic "Something went wrong" |
| rendering | through `renderForPlatform` | **raw** |

Folding `friendlyMediaError` into `formatBotError` (a thin wrapper that keeps only 413/415 media copy) means **401 / 500 / timeout / network are handled identically** whether the error came from chat, a command, or a media upload — same wording, same clickable auth link, same per-platform rendering. So media stops being a second-class error path. That's the consistency win.

## What it does NOT fix — and this is the one you actually hit

The error you saw — **"❌ Something went wrong. Please try again later."** for the dead Gemini model — comes from a different root: the backend chat stream emits `{"error": str(e)}` (the raw Python exception, `chat_service.py:475`). The bot can't classify a raw string, so **everything non-HTTP collapses to the generic fallback**. Model-not-found, DB down, a transient provider blip — the user sees the same shrug.

Consolidating the mappers doesn't change that, because the bot still only receives an opaque string.

## The extension that actually fixes general errors

It's the **same principle as the rate-limit fix** — backend emits structured data, bot maps it — applied to the error payload:

1. **Backend emits a structured error**, not `str(e)`: `{ code, message }` (reuse `AppError`, add a stable machine `code`/`type`). The SSE `{error}` event and the AppError JSON carry the same shape — exactly like unifying the 429 detail with the `rate_limit_data` card.
2. **Bot maps codes → accurate text** in the one `formatBotError`:
   - `llm_provider_error` → "GAIA's AI is temporarily unavailable — please try again in a moment." *(this is your dead-model case — becomes actionable instead of a shrug)*
   - `auth_required` → the auth prompt
   - `timeout` → "taking longer than usual…"
   - unknown → generic fallback (safe default preserved)
3. **Web benefits too** — it can show a matching specific toast from the same `code`, just like it already does for `rate_limit_data`.

So: the rate-limit fix is one instance of the pattern; structured **error codes** are the twin that upgrades *every* non-429 error from "Something went wrong" to a specific, safe, actionable message — without leaking raw exception text across the boundary.

This error-code layer is a **separate, additive chunk** (touches `chat_service` stream errors + `AppError` + the bot's `formatBotError` mapping + tests). It wasn't in the "full clean" scope I priced — that one was rate-limit-centric.

Want me to **fold the structured-error-code layer into the full-clean refactor** (so we fix general-error accuracy in the same pass), or keep the refactor rate-limit-focused and do error codes as a follow-up?

content.text:
Shipped — `8ee6dbc55`, pushed.

## What the 429 fix does
When a bot user hits the **media** upload/transcribe limit, the reply now shows the upgrade URL instead of a dead-end message:

> You've reached your **file upload** limit for now. Please try again later, or upgrade your plan for higher limits.
> `https://<your-frontend>/pricing`

- The URL comes from the bot's own `GaiaClient.getPricingUrl()` (`frontendUrl/pricing`) — bare URL, so it auto-links on every platform without markdown.
- Wording is now per-feature: **"voice transcription"** for voice notes, **"file upload"** for images/docs (the old string said "file upload" for both).
- Wired into all three media-capable adapters (Discord, Telegram, WhatsApp).

## Verified
- 368/368 tests pass (fixed the one mock that needed `getPricingUrl`)
- type-check ✓, lint ✓, dead-code scan ✓
- 6 files, committed + pushed to `feat/whatsapp-media-support`

This is the scoped fix you asked for — I did **not** touch the bigger error/rate-limit architecture (the structured-data refactor + error codes we discussed). That's still on the table as a follow-up whenever you want it; this just makes the upgrade link show up where it was missing.
